name: Jordanian Arabic (Ammani)
notes: Jordanian Arabic — Ammani koine, South Levantine but markedly Bedouin/Ḥōrāni-influenced. DISTINGUISHING FEATURE vs the Syro-Lebanese koines: ق qaf → /ɡ/ is widespread and prestigious (rural, Bedouin, and much male urban speech), alongside the urban /ʔ/ (more frequent in Ammani women's and Palestinian-origin speech); both are listed, ɡ first. ج jim → /dʒ/ (affricate retained, Bedouin pattern) more than /ʒ/. Interdentals frequently CONSERVED in Bedouin/rural Jordanian (θ, ð, ðˤ), unlike the urban Syro-Lebanese merger — but variable; the Levantine parent's merged variants remain available. Source: Holes (2004); Levantine Arabic phonology (Wikipedia); Al-Wer (2007) The formation of the dialect of Amman.

sources.author: Al-Wer, E.
sources.title: The formation of the dialect of Amman: From chaos to order
sources.notes: Sociolinguistic account of the Ammani koine: g/ʔ qaf variation by gender and origin.

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties
